description:
Make the top-level function-definition family compile through compiler macros instead of direct source publication. Source-spelled `def`/`defp`/`defmacro`/`defmacrop` should be expanded by compiler-prelude macros into canonical raw function definition fragments wrapped in `Fz.Compiler.define(..., __ENV__)`.

This ticket must preserve the staged boundary already established in compiler2: top-level definition macros decide what functions exist, but they do not expand function bodies. `FunctionSource` stays raw; `ExpandFunctionSource` remains the demand-time body-local macro/sugar pass; `DefineFunction` keeps waiting on `ExpandedFunctionSource`.

Signal: top-level function definitions enter compiler2 only through the compiler callback, `ScopeCode` does no body-local expansion for them, and demanded functions still expand exactly once behind `ExpandedFunctionSource`.
title: Compiler2 function definition macros: route def/defp/defmacro/defmacrop through the compiler callback and keep bodies lazy
